items.q: What is the worst-case complexity of bubble sort?
items.misconception: O(n) because it just passes through the list.
items.why: Students count one pass and forget that bubble sort does up to n passes, each comparing up to n elements. The nested loop structure gives n times n = n squared.

items.q: What does inheritance allow a subclass to do?
items.correct: Reuse and extend the attributes and methods of its superclass.
items.random_wrong: Copy code from any unrelated class.
items.why: Students think inheritance overrides everything. It actually REUSES the parent's members (which can be added to or overridden), creating an 'is-a' relationship.

items.q: What is the scope of a local variable?
items.correct: Accessible only within the function/block where it is declared.
items.random_wrong: Stored on a local computer rather than a server.
items.why: Students confuse 'local' with 'on this computer'. In programming, scope refers to the CODE REGION where the variable name resolves - local variables vanish when the function returns.

items.q: In the code FOR i = 1 TO 5: total = total + i, what is the final total if total starts at 0?
items.why: Correct answer: 15. “10” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which construct chooses between two paths?
items.why: Correct answer: Selection. “Sequence” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: A WHILE loop runs while x < 5. If x starts at 0 and increases by 1 each loop, how many times does the body run?
items.why: Exact answer: 5. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
